metadata.title: Production Launch — DNS, HTTPS, Backup & Storage

story.iWant: to access Ditto at a real domain with HTTPS, knowing my data is backed up and files are stored reliably
story.soThat: I can use Ditto as my primary job search tool with confidence

tasks.task: Provision and configure production host Set up Hetzner Cloud CX22 instance (Ashburn, VA) Install Docker and Docker Compose v2 on the host Configure firewall: allow ports 80, 443 (Caddy), 22 (SSH); block all others Copy production environment variables to the host (.env file) Deploy the full stack via docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d

Configure DNS and verify HTTPS Point domain A record to Hetzner host IP address Update DOMAIN env var on production host to the real domain Update CORS_ORIGINS to include the production domain Update NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXTAUTH_URL to https://<domain> Verify Caddy auto-provisions Let's Encrypt TLS certificate Verify HTTPS works end-to-end: frontend loads, /api/health returns 200 Update DEPLOY_DOMAIN GitHub Actions secret for smoke tests

Set up production S3-compatible file storage Create Cloudflare R2 bucket (or chosen S3-compatible service) for file storage Generate R2 API credentials (access key ID + secret access key) Configure production env vars: AWS_REGION=auto, AWS_S3_BUCKET, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_ENDPOINT Verify file upload flow: upload a test file via the UI, confirm it appears in R2 bucket Verify file download flow: download the test file, confirm integrity

Create database backup script Create scripts/backup-db.sh: pg_dump from Docker container, compress, upload to S3 backup bucket with timestamped name Create S3 backup bucket (or prefix in same bucket) for database dumps Implement 7-day retention: delete backups older than 7 days after successful upload Add logging to stdout for Docker log capture Set up cron job on production host: run backup script daily at 02:00 UTC Verify backup runs and dump file appears in S3

Create database restore script Create scripts/restore-db.sh: download backup from S3, stop backend, pg_restore into database, restart backend Test restore procedure: take a backup, wipe the database, restore, verify all data recovered Document the restore procedure with step-by-step instructions in script comments

Configure OAuth production callback URLs Update GitHub OAuth app: set callback URL to https://<domain>/api/auth/callback/github Update Google OAuth app: set callback URL to https://<domain>/api/auth/callback/google Set production OAuth secrets on host: AUTH_GITHUB_ID, AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET, AUTH_GOOGLE_ID, AUTH_GOOGLE_SECRET Verify GitHub login flow end-to-end on production Verify Google login flow end-to-end on production

Production deployment and smoke testing Trigger deploy via push to main (uses CI/CD pipeline from Story 7.2) Run scripts/smoke-test.sh against production domain Verify all existing features work: create application, log interview, upload file, search, export Verify notification scheduler runs in production Confirm total infrastructure cost is <$10/month

acceptanceCriteria.ac: HTTPS with valid TLS certificate — Given DNS is configured, when users visit the production domain, then they see Ditto over HTTPS with a valid TLS certificate issued by Let's Encrypt via Caddy.
Production S3-compatible file storage — Given production is running, when a file is uploaded, then it is stored in production S3-compatible storage (Cloudflare R2 or equivalent), not LocalStack.

Automated daily database backup — Given the backup cron job runs daily at 02:00 UTC, then a compressed database dump (pg_dump --format=custom --compress=9) is uploaded to S3 with naming ditto-YYYY-MM-DD.dump, and backups older than 7 days are deleted.
Database restore from backup — Given a backup exists in S3, when the restore script (scripts/restore-db.sh) runs against a fresh database, then all data is recovered correctly.

OAuth with production callback URLs — Given OAuth is configured with production callback URLs in GitHub and Google developer consoles, when users click GitHub/Google login, then the OAuth flow completes successfully.

doc.title: Technical Specification — Epic 7
doc.section: Technical Details, Backup Strategy, S3-Compatible File Storage, Configuration Changes, Implementation Guide
doc.snippet: Defines the full production Docker architecture (Caddy reverse proxy, backend, frontend, DB), daily pg_dump backup strategy with 7-day retention to S3, Cloudflare R2 as recommended S3 storage, and complete production environment variables list.

doc.title: Epic 7: CI/CD Pipeline & Production Deployment
doc.snippet: Defines Story 7.3 acceptance criteria, success criteria (HTTPS, daily backups, S3 storage, <$10/mo budget, OAuth), and story dependencies (7.2 must be complete).

doc.title: Ditto Architecture Document
doc.snippet: NextAuth v5 beta for OAuth (GitHub, Google), JWT auth with 24h/7d tokens, S3 presigned URL upload flow, CORS origins currently localhost only — needs production domain.

doc.title: Ditto Backend Architecture
doc.section: S3 Service, Authentication, CORS Configuration, Startup Sequence
doc.snippet: S3 service at internal/services/s3/service.go generates presigned URLs using AWS_* env vars. CORS configured in main.go. Health check at GET /health. Graceful shutdown via SIGINT/SIGTERM.

doc.title: Ditto Database Schema
doc.snippet: PostgreSQL 15 with UUID PKs, soft deletes, auto-timestamps, full-text search with GIN indexes. Critical context for backup/restore — pg_dump must capture all tables, triggers, indexes, and search vectors.

doc.title: Ditto Deployment Guide
doc.section: Production Environment Variables, Backup Strategy, Docker Deployment
doc.snippet: Documents backend and frontend production env vars, Docker Compose production patterns, backup strategy with pg_dump + S3 upload, and security checklist for pre-deployment.

doc.title: Story 7.1: Production Container Images & Platform Decision
doc.snippet: Created production Dockerfiles (backend ~25MB, frontend ~150MB), docker-compose.prod.yml with Caddy, Caddyfile, .env.example files. Chose Hetzner CX22 (Ashburn) at ~$5/mo. CORS_ORIGINS env var added.

doc.title: Story 7.2: CI/CD Pipeline & Automated Deployment
doc.snippet: Created ci.yml (5 parallel jobs) and deploy.yml (build-and-push → deploy). Smoke test at scripts/smoke-test.sh. GitHub Actions secrets: SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, SSH_HOST, SSH_USER, DEPLOY_DOMAIN. GITHUB_TOKEN for GHCR auth.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: NFR-7 defines production deployment requirements including automated deployment, monitoring, and backup strategy as non-functional requirements for the MVP.

file.reason: Defines all production services (db, backend, frontend, caddy). Env vars (DOMAIN, AWS_*, CORS_ORIGINS, OAuth) must be set in .env on production host. Caddy exposes ports 80/443.
Uses {$DOMAIN} env var for automatic HTTPS. Routes /health and /api/* to backend:8081, everything else to frontend:3000. DNS must point to host IP for Let's Encrypt to provision cert.
Template for production .env file. All vars (DOMAIN, DB_*, JWT_SECRET, CORS_ORIGINS, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, AUTH_SECRET, NEXTAUTH_URL, OAuth secrets, AWS_*) must be populated on production host.
CORS_ORIGINS env var parsing — when set, overrides localhost defaults. Production domain must be in CORS_ORIGINS for frontend-to-backend communication.
S3 client uses AWS_* env vars. Supports custom endpoint (cfg.Endpoint) for S3-compatible services like R2. No code changes needed — just configure env vars for R2.
Multi-stage build producing ~25MB image. Alpine base, non-root user, healthcheck via /health. Runs migrations on startup via NewAppState().
NextAuth v5 with GitHub and Google OAuth providers. Uses NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL for backend calls, AUTH_SECRET and NEXTAUTH_URL. OAuth callback URLs must match provider console settings.
Copies source to server via SCP, builds images on server, runs docker compose up. Uses DEPLOY_DOMAIN secret for smoke tests. Must update DEPLOY_DOMAIN to real domain.
Tests /health endpoint, frontend load, and HTTPS cert validity. Run against production domain after deployment to verify AC #1.
Initializes DB connection, runs migrations on startup, creates S3 service from env vars. Production startup path — all env vars must be set correctly.
Adds HSTS header when GIN_MODE=release. Production backend must run with GIN_MODE=release for HSTS to be active.

ecosystem.name: go
ecosystem.package: aws-sdk-go-v2 v1.41.0 (S3 client — used for file storage and backup uploads)
golang-migrate/migrate/v4 v4.18.3 (DB migrations — run on startup)
joho/godotenv v1.5.1 (env var loading — skips gracefully if no .env)

ecosystem.name: infrastructure
ecosystem.package: Hetzner Cloud CX22 (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM, Ashburn VA) — ~$5/mo
Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible file storage) — free tier
Let's Encrypt (TLS certificates via Caddy) — free
GitHub Actions (CI/CD) — free for public repos
GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) — free for public repos

constraints.constraint: Infrastructure budget must be <$10/month total (Hetzner CX22 ~$5/mo + Cloudflare R2 free tier)
No code changes to backend or frontend — this story is infrastructure/ops only. S3 service already supports custom endpoints via AWS_ENDPOINT env var.
Backup script must use pg_dump --format=custom --compress=9 for maximum compression and flexible restore
Backup naming convention: ditto-YYYY-MM-DD.dump in S3
Backup retention: 7 days — delete older backups after successful upload
Restore must use pg_restore --clean --if-exists to handle existing data gracefully
Caddy handles TLS termination — backend receives plain HTTP internally (sslmode=disable for Docker-internal DB connection)
Only Caddy ports (80, 443) and SSH (22) exposed to internet — all other services on internal Docker network
JWT_SECRET and AUTH_SECRET must be 64+ character random strings in production
OAuth callback URLs are manual configuration in GitHub/Google developer consoles — not automatable
deploy.yml creates frontend/.env.production on server to bake NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL into the frontend build
Scripts (backup-db.sh, restore-db.sh) must work with Docker containers — pg_dump/pg_restore via docker exec against the db container

interface.name: S3 Service Config
interface.signature: NewS3Service(cfg Config{Region, Bucket, AccessKeyID, SecretAccessKey, URLExpiry, Endpoint}) → (*S3Service, error)

interface.name: Backend OAuth Endpoint
interface.signature: POST /api/oauth → {provider, email, name, avatar_url} → {access_token, refresh_token, expires_in, user}

interface.name: Deploy Workflow
interface.signature: Push to main → SCP source → Build on server → docker compose up → smoke-test.sh

interface.name: Smoke Test
interface.signature: smoke-test.sh <base-url> → tests /health, frontend load, HTTPS cert

tests.standards: No new unit or integration tests in this story. The CI pipeline (ci.yml) runs all existing tests automatically on every push — 72% backend coverage (Go test + testify, table-driven tests) and 122 frontend tests (Jest 30.2 + React Testing Library). Production verification relies on scripts/smoke-test.sh for deployment health and manual end-to-end testing for file upload, OAuth, and backup/restore procedures.

ideas.idea: Run smoke-test.sh against production domain — verify /health returns 200, frontend loads, HTTPS cert valid
Verify HSTS header present in production responses (GIN_MODE=release enables it)
Upload a file via UI on production, verify it appears in Cloudflare R2 bucket dashboard
Download the uploaded file, verify integrity matches original
Manually trigger backup-db.sh, verify .dump file appears in S3 with correct naming (ditto-YYYY-MM-DD.dump)
Verify backup file is a valid pg_dump custom format (pg_restore --list should succeed)
Wait 24h or adjust retention, verify old backups are cleaned up
Take a backup, drop/recreate database, run restore-db.sh, verify all data recovered by browsing the app
After restore, verify search vectors and full-text search still work (triggers rebuild tsvector on data load)
Click GitHub login on production, complete OAuth flow, verify user session created
Click Google login on production, complete OAuth flow, verify user session created
Full end-to-end: create application, log interview, upload file, search, export — all on production domain
